extraction_note: Two ingestion paths in extract_web.py: (1) DBAASP REST JSON peptide cards; (2) DRAMP antimicrobial-activity bulk Excel at `data/raw/web/Antimicrobial.xlsx` (official site file `Antimicrobial_amps.xlsx`; Target_Organism `(MIC …)` parsing). Dependencies: requests, pandas, openpyxl. Many DRAMP rows overlap primary literature/DBAASP — deduplicate at merge/clean if needed. Per-source caps: max_records and source-specific keys below. Every exported row must have non-empty peptide_sequence and measurement_value (verbatim MIC token).

input_pages.robots_notes: Public JSON at https://dbaasp.org/peptides?page=&size=. Throttle ≥1 req/s (script default).
input_pages.selection_reason: Primary structured MIC per peptide–target with medium/inoculum when available.

parser_plan.filter: Monomers only; MIC activity measure; bacterial targets.
parser_plan.notes: verbatim MIC text; canonical units via scripts/utils.py only.

input_pages.snapshot_note: Pagination index only (page, totalCount, count per API list request); full peptide cards are fetched at extraction time and written to web_extracted_records.csv.

input_pages.access_method: Download `Antimicrobial_amps.xlsx` from DRAMP 4.0 bulk downloads (https://dramp.cpu-bioinfor.org/downloads/; legacy path segment `DRAMP3.0_new` in the direct URL). Save unchanged copy as local_workbook_path `data/raw/web/Antimicrobial.xlsx`. Columns include DRAMP_ID, Sequence, Name, Source, Activity, Target_Organism, Pubmed_ID.
input_pages.notes: Substantial overlap with DBAASP/literature is expected — use source_id + sequence + pathogen when deduplicating.

parser_plan.filter: Activity contains Antibacterial; Target_Organism must contain `(MIC …)` clauses parseable by shared regex helpers.
